meta_title: Roofing in Sunshine Ranches, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Sunshine Ranches, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, A+ BBB. Free quotes – call today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacements & Repairs for Sunshine Ranches Homes
intro:
Sunshine Ranches is one of Broward County's most distinctive communities — a rare pocket of equestrian estates, sprawling lots, and luxury ranch-style homes tucked between Southwest Ranches, Davie, and Weston. With average home values hovering around $900,000, a roof isn't just a structural component here — it's a defining feature of your property's curb appeal, resale value, and long-term protection. Whether you own a sprawling estate near the Sunshine Ranches Equestrian Park or a custom-built home in Sunshine Ranches Estates, your roof faces real and persistent threats from South Florida's volatile weather patterns. Choosing the right roofing contractor isn't just a maintenance decision — it's an investment in one of your most valuable assets.

Despite sitting approximately 14 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Sunshine Ranches is far from immune to hurricane-force conditions. The area falls within a 140 mph wind zone under Florida Building Code classifications, meaning every new or replacement roof must meet strict engineering and product standards. Broward County's storm history is sobering: since 1990, the region has experienced eight significant storm events, including the catastrophic Hurricane Andrew in 1992, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017. Each of these storms exposed weaknesses in older roofing systems, particularly on the roughly 20% of Sunshine Ranches homes built before 1994 — before modern hurricane-resistant building codes took effect. For these older properties especially, a roof replacement isn't optional maintenance; it's a critical safety and financial priority.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our reputation as South Florida's Kings of Service by combining premium roofing materials with 100% in-house installation and a team that carries over 60 years of combined experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We understand Sunshine Ranches — its unique mix of architectural styles, its permitting process through the Town of Southwest Ranches Building Department, and the expectations of homeowners who demand nothing short of excellence. When we put a new roof on your home, we're not just protecting it from the next storm; we're enhancing its value, reducing your insurance premiums, and giving you lasting peace of mind.

section_why.h2: Why Sunshine Ranches Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Needs
section_why.body:
Not every roofing company is equipped to serve a market like Sunshine Ranches. The homes here are larger, often architecturally complex, and set on expansive properties where the roofline is as much about aesthetics as it is about function. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed over 500 homes across South Florida, and our experience with high-value estate properties in Broward County gives us a distinct advantage when it comes to planning, executing, and finishing roofing projects of this caliber. We don't subcontract our work — every installation is performed by our own trained, licensed crews, which means tighter quality control and direct accountability to you from the first inspection to the final nail.

Our in-house approach matters more than homeowners often realize. Many roofing companies in South Florida rely heavily on subcontractors, which introduces variables in workmanship, scheduling, and warranty responsibility. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the team that shows up to your Sunshine Ranches home is the team we train, supervise, and stand behind. Our project managers are on-site throughout the process, ensuring that every layer — from the decking and underlayment to the final course of shingles or tile — meets both manufacturer specifications and Florida Building Code requirements for Broward County's 140 mph wind zone.

We're also deeply familiar with the permitting process specific to this part of Broward County. Roofing permits in Sunshine Ranches are processed through the Town of Southwest Ranches Building Department, and navigating that process correctly requires knowledge of local code interpretations and inspection requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permit pulling, scheduling, and inspections on your behalf, so you never have to worry about compliance or certificate of occupancy issues down the road. Our A+ BBB rating and long-standing relationships with local inspectors are a direct reflection of how seriously we take this responsibility.

Finally, we understand that homeowners in Sunshine Ranches Estates and surrounding areas have high expectations — and rightfully so. You've invested significantly in your property, and you deserve a roofing partner who matches that investment with premium products, expert craftsmanship, and genuine customer service. That's what being the Kings of Service means to us: showing up, doing the job right, and making sure you're completely satisfied long after the last inspection is passed.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Proves Why Sunshine Ranches Needs Serious Roof Protection
section_history.body:
Sunshine Ranches has been tested repeatedly by South Florida's hurricane history, and the lessons written by those storms are etched into the community's building landscape. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a defining moment for all of Broward and Miami-Dade — a Category 5 event that exposed just how vulnerable older roofing systems were to extreme wind loads. While Andrew's epicenter was further south, Broward County experienced significant wind and structural damage, and many pre-1994 homes in the Sunshine Ranches area still carry the legacy of that storm in outdated roofing systems that may have been patched rather than properly replaced in the decades since.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 brought a different kind of destruction to Broward County — a fast-moving but intensely powerful storm that produced some of the highest wind gusts ever recorded in the region. Wilma caused widespread roof damage across Broward, stripping shingles, cracking tile, and compromising underlayment systems on thousands of homes. For Sunshine Ranches homeowners with aging roofs, Wilma was often the storm that revealed deferred maintenance issues and accelerated the need for full replacements. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly over South Florida and caused billions in insured losses across the tri-county area. Irma reinforced that no inland Broward community — including those 14 miles from the coast — is truly safe from hurricane-force wind damage.

With eight significant storm events since 1990, Sunshine Ranches homeowners face a statistical reality: your roof will be tested again. The question isn't whether a storm will come; it's whether your roof will be ready when it does. A properly installed, hurricane-rated roofing system from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can mean the difference between a clean inspection certificate and a catastrophic claim — and between a home that retains its $900,000 value and one that suffers structural compromise.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Sunshine Ranches and Broward County
section_code.body:
Sunshine Ranches sits within a 140 mph wind speed zone as defined by the Florida Building Code, which places serious performance requirements on any roofing system installed in the area. While Sunshine Ranches is not classified as part of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies to Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closer to the coast — the area still requires hurricane-rated products and installation methods that meet or exceed the 2023 Florida Building Code, Residential volume. This distinction matters because it affects which products are approved, how they must be installed, and what documentation must accompany permit applications.

All roofing replacements in Sunshine Ranches require a permit issued through the Town of Southwest Ranches Building Department. This process includes submission of product approval numbers (issued by the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation), proof of contractor licensing and insurance, and compliance with local wind load tables. Inspections are required at multiple stages — typically at the decking level and upon completion — and a final inspection sign-off is required before the permit can be closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every step of this process for our clients, from initial permit application through final inspection, ensuring zero gaps in code compliance.

For homes built before 1994, code compliance on a reroofing project can also trigger requirements for upgrading the roof deck attachment — specifically, ensuring that the sheathing is properly fastened using the spacing and nail size required by current code. This is not just a bureaucratic hurdle; proper deck attachment is one of the most critical factors in a roof's ability to survive high-wind events. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's crews are trained to identify and address these conditions as part of every full replacement, so your completed roof is not just beautiful — it's fully code-compliant and built to last.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems Built for Sunshine Ranches
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading roofing systems that are ideally matched to the climate, architecture, and performance expectations of Sunshine Ranches homeowners: CertainTeed architectural shingles from the Landmark series and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines are engineered to meet Florida's demanding wind and weather requirements while delivering the aesthetic quality that high-value estate properties deserve.

CertainTeed's Landmark series is one of the most recognized and trusted architectural shingle systems in North America, and for good reason. These laminated, dimensional shingles replicate the rich depth of wood shake or slate at a fraction of the cost, while delivering wind resistance ratings up to 130 mph with standard installation — and up to 150 mph with enhanced fastening methods. For Sunshine Ranches homes in a 140 mph wind zone, Landmark shingles installed using the enhanced protocol provide a performance margin that meets code and then some. CertainTeed also backs the Landmark series with a Lifetime Limited Warranty and a SureStart Protection period that covers full replacement costs in the early years of the product's life. The shingles are also Impact Resistance Class 4 eligible when specified, which can unlock additional insurance discounts.

For homeowners seeking the timeless elegance of tile — a hallmark of South Florida luxury architecture — Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs West Lake concrete and clay tile systems that are engineered specifically for high-wind environments. West Lake tiles carry Florida Product Approval and are tested for uplift resistance in wind exposures consistent with Broward County's requirements. Whether you prefer the smooth barrel profile of clay tile or the dimensional look of concrete tile in slate, wood shake, or flat profiles, West Lake offers a range of options that complement the ranch-style and Mediterranean architectural designs common throughout Sunshine Ranches Estates. Tile roofs, when properly installed, can last 40 to 50 years — making them an exceptional long-term value for homeowners who plan to stay in their property for decades.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Sunshine Ranches — and How Do You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
Given the size and complexity of homes in Sunshine Ranches, roofing project costs will vary, but homeowners should generally expect a full roof replacement to range from approximately $18,000 to $45,000 or more depending on the square footage, roofline complexity, material selection, and any deck repairs required. For the large estate homes that characterize this community — many exceeding 3,000 to 5,000 square feet of living space with proportionally larger roof surfaces — investing in a high-quality roofing system is a straightforward financial decision when weighed against the home's $900,000 average value. A premium CertainTeed Landmark or West Lake tile roof doesn't just protect the structure; it enhances the property's marketability and can command a measurable premium at resale.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options designed to make roof replacement accessible without compromising on quality. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. For a project of this significance, being able to spread payments over time while your new roof is already protecting and appreciating your home is a smart financial move. We work with multiple lending partners to find the right program for your situation, and our team can walk you through options during your free estimate appointment.

Beyond financing, the return on investment from a new roof in Sunshine Ranches is compelling from multiple angles. A properly documented, hurricane-rated roof replacement — complete with a wind mitigation inspection report — can unlock significant insurance premium reductions through credits for features like secondary water resistance (SWR) layers, roof deck attachment upgrades, and roof covering ratings. In Broward County, homeowners have seen annual insurance savings ranging from hundreds to several thousand dollars per year after a qualifying roof replacement and wind mitigation report. Over 10 to 15 years, those savings can offset a substantial portion of the original roofing investment.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance in Sunshine Ranches
section_insurance.body:
Homeowners insurance in South Florida — and Broward County specifically — has become one of the most significant ongoing costs for property owners. Premiums have surged dramatically in recent years as carriers respond to increased storm losses, reinsurance costs, and widespread roof-related claims. For Sunshine Ranches homeowners with older roofs — particularly those on homes built before 1994 — insurance renewals can come with steep increases, coverage limitations, or even non-renewal notices. A new, hurricane-rated roof installed by a licensed contractor like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is one of the most powerful tools available to stabilize and reduce your insurance costs.

The key mechanism for insurance savings on a new roof is the wind mitigation inspection. Conducted by a licensed inspector after your new roof is installed, a wind mitigation report documents specific features of your roof system — including deck attachment method and spacing, roof shape, secondary water resistance underlayment, and roof covering type and rating. Each qualifying attribute earns credits that Florida law requires insurance carriers to apply to your premium. Homes with roofs that achieve the highest ratings on all attributes can see reductions of 30% to 50% or more on their wind portion of the premium — which for a high-value Sunshine Ranches home can translate to thousands of dollars in annual savings.

Many major carriers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — have become increasingly strict about roof age and condition as underwriting criteria. A roof older than 15 to 20 years can disqualify a home from preferred carrier placement, forcing owners into higher-cost options. By replacing your roof with a certified hurricane-rated system through Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you not only meet carrier requirements — you position your home for the most favorable underwriting terms available. Our team can advise you on which product specifications and documentation to request so your wind mitigation report yields the maximum possible insurance benefit.

faqs.q: Does my Sunshine Ranches home need a new roof, or can it be repaired?
faqs.a: The answer depends on your roof's age, material, and the extent of existing damage. For homes built before 1994 — about 20% of Sunshine Ranches — a full replacement is often the most cost-effective path, especially when considering insurance eligibility and code compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections to assess your specific roof's condition honestly and give you a clear recommendation without pressure. Repairs are a great solution for isolated storm damage on a newer, otherwise sound roof, while full replacements make more sense for aging systems with widespread wear.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Sunshine Ranches, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Full roof replacements for Sunshine Ranches homes typically range from $18,000 to $45,000 or more depending on roof size, material choice, and any deck repairs needed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. During your free estimate, our team will walk you through product options at multiple price points and help you identify the financing plan that makes the most sense for your budget and long-term goals.

faqs.q: What permits are required for a roof replacement in Sunshine Ranches?
faqs.a: All roofing replacements in Sunshine Ranches require a permit from the Town of Southwest Ranches Building Department. The permit application must include Florida Product Approval documentation for the roofing system, contractor licensing and insurance verification, and compliance with Broward County's 140 mph wind zone requirements. Multiple inspections are required during and after installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process — from application to final inspection sign-off — so you don't have to navigate it alone.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Sunshine Ranches?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements on Sunshine Ranches homes are completed within two to five days of active installation, depending on the size of the roof, the material selected, and whether any deck repairs are needed. Tile roofs may take slightly longer than shingle systems due to the installation process. The full project timeline — including permitting, material delivery, installation, and final inspection — typically spans two to six weeks from contract signing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps you informed at every stage so you always know what to expect.

faqs.q: Will a new roof lower my homeowners insurance premium in Sunshine Ranches?
faqs.a: Yes — in most cases, significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof combined with a wind mitigation inspection report can unlock substantial insurance credits in Florida. Credits are applied for qualifying features like enhanced deck attachment, secondary water resistance underlayment, and high-wind-rated roof coverings. For a high-value Sunshine Ranches property, these credits can translate to annual savings of hundreds to several thousand dollars. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs products and methods specifically chosen to maximize wind mitigation credit eligibility, and we can advise you on what documentation to request from your inspector.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Sunshine Ranches Home with a Premium New Roof?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roofing inspection and estimate — our Kings of Service team is ready to deliver the craftsmanship and peace of mind your Sunshine Ranches home deserves.
